text: REST APIs are commonly used for building web services. When designing them, you should follow some best practices to make them easy to use. First, use clear and logical endpoints, like `/users` or `/orders`. Each endpoint should represent a specific resource. Use standard HTTP status codes to communicate results: 200 for success, 404 for not found, and 500 for server errors. Always include documentation that explains how to use the API, including examples. Finally, plan for versioning from the start, for example by adding a version number to the URL like `/v1/users`. This helps you make changes without breaking existing users' applications.
